items.title: Testing reveals popular weight loss peptide had double the strength on label

children.content: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

gallery.altText: A vial of liquid sitting on a table with a label saying 'retatrutide 10mg'

thumbnail.altText: A vial of liquid sitting on a table with a label saying 'retatrutide 10mg'

items.title: Why hasn't the global economy completely collapsed?

children.content: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

image.altText: Donald Trump smiles while looking to the side.

items.title: Trump calls Hormuz drone strike 'foolish' breach of ceasefire

children.content: The US president takes to Truth Social to criticise an Iranian drone attack on a cargo ship earlier this week.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands at a podium with US flags in the background.

items.title: Defence home loan subsidy to change after veterans left worse off

children.content: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

image.altText: Medals pinned to a soldier's uniform.

items.title: Venezuela's quake began with a 'soft shake' and then the lights went out

children.content: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

image.altText: A man involved in rescue efforts looks on from a damaged building in Venezuela.

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

gallery.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
Hundreds of pelicans sitting down on flat, brown ground
An aerial photo of Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre

thumbnail.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
Hundreds of pelicans sitting down on flat, brown ground
An aerial photo of Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre

items.title: John Williamson on what it means to be True Blue in 2026

children.content: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

image.altText: Man leaning against wooden fence with a guitar

items.title: Andrew 'Twiggy' Forrest warns Fortescue staff after sexual harassment class action

children.content: In an email sent to all staff, Mr Forrest said: "Anyone who thinks this behaviour has a place at Fortescue is in the wrong company."

image.altText: Andrew Forrest in high-vis shirt and white hard hat folds his arms across his chest, October 24, 2025.

articles.title: The Socceroos are through but they still have a big problem to solve

children.content: The Socceroos are into the knockout rounds of the World Cup, but they still have a big problem to solve.

image.altText: Irvine and Behich hug, a smile on Irvine's face.

articles.title: Socceroos heap praise on 'best player' Bos after knockouts progression

children.content: Jordy Bos may not have been named player of the match, but another strong performance, in which he was asked to play on the right of defence instead of his preferred left, was lauded by his teammates.

image.altText: Jordan Bos of Australia leads the crowd in a cheer after the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Top spot in Group I goes on the line as France and Norway lock horns, though we have to wait for superstars Erling Haaland and Kylian Mbappé to clash. Follow live.

items.title: 'Feels like we've been scammed': Graduates unhappy with uni degrees

children.content: Australian students are the least satisfied they have ever been with their university degrees, according to a long-running survey, leaving some questioning the value of their qualifications.

items.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building

children.content: Two bystanders have said an aircraft about the size of a car has crashed into Beijing's CITIC Tower.

items.title: Broncos coach rejects claim season is done despite seventh straight loss

children.content: Queensland halfback Sam Walker is put on report for a shoulder charge in the Roosters' 24-18 win over Brisbane, potentially ruling him out of State of Origin III.

image.altText: Payne Haas on his haunches after a Brisbane Broncos loss.

items.title: Giants 'obliterated' at stoppage as Hawks win at MCG on night of injuries

children.content: Hawks hold on at the MCG to beat GWS and lock up third spot for the moment, in a game marred by a number of big injuries.

image.altText: Two Hawthorn AFL players put their arms around each other in celebration after a goal.

items.title: When you buy merch at a gig, bands aren't making as much as you'd think

items.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics

children.content: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

items.title: Hundreds killed as Venezuela struck by 'doublet' earthquake

children.content: The deadly one-two punch that toppled buildings in Venezuela's capital and beyond was a doublet earthquake, a pair of similar-sized quakes close in location and time.

items.title: Former cricketer pleads guilty to underage sexual abuse charges

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

items.title: Reaction to Karl Stefanovic leaving Channel Nine

children.content: Veteran Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.
Nine today confirmed it would part ways with Stefanovic "effective immediately".
Here is what some people thought of it.
